title: 11 Herbal Creams For Puffy Eyes
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to reduce puffy eyes and look more refreshed. So, why do they work? Well, when you drink herbal teas, the active ingredients are absorbed into your bloodstream and then reach the delicate skin around your eyes. This helps to reduce swelling and ease any discomfort.  One of the key herbs to look for is Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera. It's a natural anti-inflammatory that can help to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin. Another herb you might find in eye creams is Calendula officinalis, which is known for its calming properties. It can help to reduce redness and promote healthy skin cell growth.  Echinacea purpurea is another herb that's often used in herbal creams for puffy eyes. It's a natural antioxidant that can help to protect your skin from damage and reduce inflammation. When you use a cream that contains these herbs, you can expect to see a reduction in puffiness and a more refreshed look. This can be a big boost to your self-confidence, and it can even help you to feel more energized and alert.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains a mix of active constituents, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and zinc. These ingredients help to reduce puffiness by improving circulation and reducing water retention. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera, specifically the presence of aloin and aloe-emodin, also help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes. Additionally, the gel-like consistency of aloe vera allows it to penetrate deep into the skin, providing long-lasting hydration and reducing the appearance of dark circles. By reducing inflammation and improving hydration, aloe barbadensis creams can help to make the skin around the eyes look smoother and more refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1 tablespoon of glycerin, 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel in a bowl.
Mix the distilled water and glycerin in the bowl until the glycerin dissolves.
Add the vitamin E oil and Aloe barbadensis gel to the bowl. Mix until smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a clean container and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
After chilling, use an electric mixer to whip the mixture until it becomes light and fluffy. Use immediately or store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins and sesquiterpene lactones as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness around the eyes. The antiseptic properties of sesquiterpene lactones also help to prevent infections that can cause puffiness. Calendula's ability to improve blood flow and reduce fluid retention also contributes to its effectiveness in reducing puffy eyes. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate puffy eyes and leave them looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis oil, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt Calendula officinalis oil, coconut oil, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove from heat and stir in shea butter and vitamin E oil until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify.
Once set, your Calendula cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to puffy eyes and gently massage until absorbed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains the active constituents alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. Alkylamides help reduce puffiness by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation. Glycosides, particularly echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the skin around the eyes. Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid, have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage and promote collagen production. By reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, Echinacea purpurea creams can help decrease the appearance of puffy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Add beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Heat until the beeswax melts.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Pour it into a container and let it cool completely.
Once cooled, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount of the cream and apply it to your puffy eyes. Massage gently and leave it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing off.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin and hypericin, two active compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce puffiness by decreasing the swelling and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes. The antihistamine properties of hyperforin also help alleviate eye puffiness caused by allergic reactions. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of hypericin protect the skin from damage and promote healthy skin regeneration. By reducing inflammation, soothing the skin, and promoting skin health, Hypericum perforatum creams can provide relief from puffy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum extract, 2 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, add the shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the Hypericum perforatum extract and vitamin E oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar, and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use a spatula to scoop out a small amount and apply it to puffy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams contains tannins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Tannins help reduce puffiness by shrinking blood vessels and constricting the skin, while flavonoids protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The astringent properties of tannins also help reduce swelling and ease redness, making it easier to get rid of puffy eyes. Flavonoids, on the other hand, improve blood circulation and help remove excess fluids that cause puffiness. By combining these properties, Hamamelis virginiana creams can provide long-lasting relief from puffy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Hamamelis virginiana flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Add the dried Hamamelis virginiana flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and pour it into a clean container. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds help to increase blood flow to the skin, reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba creams, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The terpenoids, including bilobalide and ginkgolides, also contribute to improved circulation and reduced fluid retention. As a result, using Ginkgo biloba creams can help to reduce the appearance of puffy eyes and leave the skin looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba extract, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from heat and add Ginkgo biloba extract, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solidified, use a spatula to scoop out a small amount of the cream and apply it to puffy eyes, gently massaging until absorbed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid and 1,8-cineole as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce puffiness in the eyes. Rosmarinic acid constricts blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation and swelling, while 1,8-cineole soothes and calms the skin. Additionally, melissa officinalis creams contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that further contribute to their effectiveness in reducing puffiness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, melissa officinalis creams can help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 teaspoons of dried Melissa officinalis leaves, and 2 drops of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the distilled water with Melissa officinalis leaves by letting it steep in a saucepan for 10-15 minutes over low heat. Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and olive oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Combine the infused water with the melted wax mixture and stir well. Add vitamin E oil and mix until combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool and harden. Store in a cool, dry place and apply a small amount to puffy eyes as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce puffiness in the eyes by decreasing inflammation and fighting free radicals that cause water retention. Rosmariinic acid constricts blood vessels, which reduces swelling and the appearance of puffy eyes. Carnosic acid also has a cooling effect that can help soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of puffiness. By reducing inflammation and water retention, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can help give the eyes a more refreshed and rested look.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the distilled water and stir well.
Stir in the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil and pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate as active constituents, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce puffiness by constricting blood vessels and preventing fluid accumulation in the eyes. The calming and soothing effects of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to relax the surrounding muscles, reducing swelling and promoting a more even appearance. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of these constituents protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. By combining these properties, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to reduce puffiness and leave the eyes looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia (lavender) flowers.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave (10-second intervals). Stir until smooth.
Add 1 cup of distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well. Then, add 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the mixture.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Stir in 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil (optional).
Pour the mixture into small containers or a tin. Let it cool and harden completely (about 30 minutes). Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains several bioactive constituents, including quinine and other alkaloids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness around the eyes. The alkaloids in Cinchona officinalis also have a vasoconstrictive effect, narrowing the blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation in the tissue. This vasoconstrictive effect helps to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. By reducing inflammation and fluid accumulation, Cinchona officinalis creams can help to give the appearance of smoother, more refreshed-looking eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/2 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 2 tablespoons Cinchona officinalis extract, and 10 drops vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add the distilled water, Cinchona officinalis extract, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let cool completely. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply to puffy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams contains antioxidants and flavonoids such as anthocyanins and quercetin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. The anthocyanins in Vaccinium myrtillus creams also have vasoconstrictive properties, which help decrease blood flow to the area and reduce the appearance of dark circles. Quercetin, on the other hand, has a soothing effect on the skin and helps to reduce redness and inflammation. By using Vaccinium myrtillus creams, you can benefit from the combination of these anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to help alleviate puffy eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium myrtillus herbal creams for puffy eyes
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Vaccinium myrtillus berries, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Mash the Vaccinium myrtillus berries in a bowl to release their juice. Set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat, stirring until combined.
Add the mashed Vaccinium myrtillus berries and vitamin E oil to the saucepan. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool. Pour the mixture into a container and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce puffy eyes. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can help reduce swelling. The warmth of the tea can also loosen up fluids that cause puffiness. Staying hydrated by drinking tea may also help flush out excess fluids from the body.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help reduce puffiness in the eyes, but consuming them daily may not be entirely safe. Some herbs like chamomile and peppermint can be beneficial, but others like sage and wormwood can be too potent. It's best to limit your intake to a few times a week and monitor how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like chamomile, peppermint, and calendula can help with puffy eyes. You might start seeing results in a few hours, depending on the severity of puffiness. Some people notice improvement within 30 minutes to an hour, while others may need a few hours.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for puffy eyes can be most effective in the morning after waking up. This is because your body has been resting overnight, and the tea can help reduce swelling and refresh your eyes. The caffeine-free brew can also give you a gentle energy boost to start your day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for puffy eyes. Some teas, like chamomile and ginkgo biloba, can increase bleeding risk or lower blood pressure. If you're taking medications for puffy eyes, be cautious with teas that may affect your condition or interact with your treatment.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some can affect your unborn baby's health. While puffy eyes are common, herbal teas like chamomile or peppermint might help. However, check the ingredients and look for teas specifically labeled as "safe for pregnancy." It's also a good idea to avoid teas with unknown or potential risks.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful remedy for puffy eyes, but it's essential to know what's safe to consume while breastfeeding. Caffeine-free teas like peppermint, chamomile, and lavender are generally considered safe. However, some teas like sage, dill, and parsley may have a small amount of estrogen, which could potentially affect milk production. If you're unsure, look for breast-friendly certifications on the tea packaging.
title_2: 11 Natural Herbal Creams for Older Adults with Puffy Eyes
